title: Choyd
content:
{{Top|leg}} {{Character |type=Criminal |image=[[File:Choyd.jpg]] |name=Choyd |homeworld=[[Vorzyd V/Legends|Vorzyd V]]"[[Gambler's World]]" |birth= |death= |species=[[Selonian/Legends|Selonian]]''[[Geonosis and the Outer Rim Worlds]]'' |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]] |pronouns=new |height= |mass= |hair=Brown |feathers= |eyes=Green |skin= |cyber= |families=new |parents=new |partners=new |siblings=new |children=new |affiliation=[[Freelies]] |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|My sire ''asked'' for it... when he joined the Rebels!|Choyd|}} '''Choyd''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Selonian/Legends|Selonian]] who was a member of the [[Freelies]] gang on his [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of [[Vorzyd V/Legends|Vorzyd V]] during the era of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. Although his father [[Falud]] was a committed [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] operative, Choyd had little care for the battles between the Alliance and the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Empire]], staying loyal to his gang. In fact, in [[1 ABY/Legends|1 ABY]]—shortly after his father's death attempting to escape from Imperial custody—Choyd even assisted in a plot to kidnap prominent Rebel leader [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]]. Although the Freelies managed to capture Organa, she ultimately escaped when a team fo Imperial [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]] laid siege to their compound. ==Biography== {{Quote|You're going against everything your father believed in, Choyd!|Leia Organa|}} A member of the relatively small [[Selonian/Legends|Selonian]] community on the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] [[Planet/Legends|world]] of [[Vorzyd V/Legends|Vorzyd V]], Choyd was the son of [[Falud]], a [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] operative on Vorzyd V during the era of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. However, Choyd did not share his father's principles, and by [[1 ABY/Legends|1 ABY]] he had instead fallen in with the [[Freelies]], a delinquent youth gang led by a [[Human/Legends|Human]] named [[Jax (Freelie)|Jax]]. Choyd and his crew were known to patrol the streets of Vorzyd V's [[Capital/Legends|capital]] [[City/Legends|city]] of [[Efavan/Legends|Efavan]], looking for things to steal: on one of these patrols they happened across a seemingly valuable pair of lost [[Droid/Legends|droids]], the [[Protocol droid/Legends|protocol droid]] [[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]] and the [[Astromech droid/Legends|astromech droid]] [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]]. Unbeknownst to them, the droids were owned by [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]], a Rebel hero who was acquainted with Choyd's father. Choyd and his friends immediately tried to claim the droids for their own, only for R2-D2 to summon a [[Police droid/Legends|police droid]] and send the Freelies scattering. [[File:ChoydJax.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Choyd and Jax.]] Still coveting the droids, the Freelies briefly encountered them again at a weapons shop, but were discouraged from taking them there when their targets inadvertently activated several automatic weapons. Instead, the youths followed them back to Skywalker's [[Luke Skywalker's starship (Tatooine)|ship]], where they wound up stealing something much more valuable. Choyd and his friends tricked C-3PO into allowing them aboard, where they kidnapped Rebel leader [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]], bringing her back to their hideout intending to ransom her to the Alliance for 5 million [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]]. Once Organa learned that Choyd was Falud's son, she passed on an important piece of information: she, Skywalker and Falud had only days earlier been in [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] custody together, and Choyd's father had been [[Death/Legends|killed]] helping them escape. Organa found herself appalled at Choyd's dispassionate response: the young Selonian did not seem to particularly care about his father's death, chalking it up to just being what Falud got for stirring things up against the Empire. Organa wasn't in the Freelies' den long before the situation got hairy. Organa's friend Skywalker soon arrived with a [[Vorzydiak]] Freelie named [[Bemmie]], whom Skywalker had rescued from an Imperial [[Torture/Legends|torture]] squad. Although Bemmie attempted to convince his compatriots that Skywalker was trustworthy, Jax refused to buy it, choosing instead to dispose of the Rebel leader. However, before he could do so, the Freelies' hideout was stormed by a team of [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]] who were after Organa and Skywalker. Choyd and his friends returned fire, although Jax planned to contact the leader of the Imperial troops and sell the two Rebels to them. But they failed to account for Bemmie, who still felt that he owed Skywalker his life. While Choyd and the other Freelies battled the Imperials, Bemmie led the Rebels to a secret escape tunnel and to safety, an act which cost him his life. ==Personality and traits== {{Quote|Falud died fighting for freedom... trying to overthrow a despicable tyranny!"
"Then he wasted it! The Empire's still around!|Leia Organa and Choyd|}} Although Choyd's father Falud was a freedom fighter who was willing to give his life for the Rebel cause, he did not pass on those same principles to his son Choyd. Instead, the younger Selonian had the disaffected, nihilistic attitude characteristic of the Freelies, which extended even to the point where Choyd had little emotional response to his father's death. Choyd saw little point in attempting to oppose the Galactic Empire, and in his eyes, his father was gone and the Empire was still in power, so Falud's death was wasted. For Choyd, only one thing mattered: the Freelies, and as long as no one bothered his gang, what the Empire did was none of his concern. Choyd was extremely loyal to the group's leader, Jax, following his orders without question. ==Behind the scenes== Choyd made his first and only appearance in the [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] continuity in ''[[Gambler's World]]'', a [[Star Wars (comic strip)|comic strip]] story arc written and illustrated by [[Russ Manning]] and released in [[1979]]. ==Appearances== *"[[Gambler's World]]" {{Reprint|Gambler's World}} {{1st}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |nl=Choyd }} [[Category:Freelies]] [[Category:Males]] [[Category:Selonians]]
